description:
The memory-first coding agent

Letta Code is a memory-first coding harness, designed for long-lived agents that can learn from experience.
Instead of working in independent sessions, you work with a persisted agent whose memory is portable across models (Claude, GPT, Gemini, GLM, Kimi, and more).
What's Changed
- refactor(slack): progress rides the assistant status line; markdown replies with web footnotes [LET-9559] by @cpacker in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/3279
- feat(reflection): a/b eval setup by @amysguan in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/3250
- refactor(channels): add maintainability guardrails by @cpacker in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/3295
- feat(models): add GPT-5.6 ChatGPT OAuth presets by @AriWebb in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/3297
- feat(local): add GPT-5.6 model support by @just-cameron in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/3294
- feat: add OpenAI-compatible provider connection by @AriWebb in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/3283
- chore: bump version to 0.28.0 by @github-actions[bot] in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/3298
Full Changelog: https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/compare/v0.27.30...v0.28.0
